title: Palabras que impresionan
desc: Words that mark a refined speaker

sn.s: Hay palabras que, bien usadas, revelan un dominio profundo.
sn.e: There are words that, used well, reveal deep command.

sn.s: Algo propio de la esencia de una cosa es 'inherente'.
sn.e: Something belonging to a thing's essence is 'inherent'.

sn.s: "El riesgo es inherente a todo cambio," digo.
sn.e: "Risk is inherent to all change," I say.

sn.s: Un resumen breve y claro es 'sucinto'.
"Seré sucinto," anuncio antes de hablar poco y bien.
sn.e: "I'll be succinct," I announce before speaking briefly and well.

sn.s: Algo necesario que no se puede evitar es 'ineludible'.
sn.e: Something necessary that can't be avoided is 'unavoidable'.

sn.s: "Tu esfuerzo es loable," me dice la profesora.
sn.e: "Your effort is laudable," the professor tells me.
Something relevant to the topic is 'pertinent'.

sn.s: 'Soslayar' es evitar o esquivar un asunto.
sn.e: 'Soslayar' is to sidestep or dodge a matter.
"I don't want to sidestep the problem," I clarify.

sn.s: Estas palabras son el sello de quien domina el idioma.
sn.e: These words are the hallmark of one who masters the language.

sn.s: Y ahora forman parte de mi vocabulario natural.
sn.e: And now they're part of my natural vocabulary.
